release_lifecycle.support_statement: Platform preview release. Supported for evaluation, documented theoremOS install flows, hybrid BIOS+UEFI boot, and the published FreeBSD upgrade contract.
release_lifecycle.security_support: Best-effort fixes apply only to the latest published preview line. Older preview lines may be superseded without backport commitments.
release_lifecycle.eol_action: When this preview line is superseded or revoked, operators must upgrade to the next supported preview line or re-image using the documented recovery path.

release_channel.name: preview
release_channel.promotion_policy: A preview release may publish only after conformance evidence, release notes, and release metadata succeed.
release_channel.freeze_policy: Preview release tags freeze contract surfaces and bundled artifacts at tag cut; any fix requires a new tag and a fresh release session.

support_tiers.test_depth: Installer, upgrade-bundle, rollback-candidate visibility, and release-contract validation are blocking.
support_tiers.security_servicing: Best-effort security servicing on the latest preview line only.
support_tiers.deprecation_policy: Removal requires an explicit release-contract update and a superseding primary target.

support_tiers.test_depth: Direct-write image boot, management server reachability, DHCP acquisition.
support_tiers.security_servicing: Best-effort security servicing on the latest preview line only.
support_tiers.deprecation_policy: Removal requires an explicit release-contract update and a superseding primary target.

paths.state_strategy: preserve theorem/store, theorem/log, and /var/lib/theorem across boot-environment switch
paths.rollback_strategy: activate previous boot environment via beadm/bectl and reboot
paths.operator_guide: Run upgrade.sh from the upgrade-bundle archive on a running 0.5.x system.

paths.state_strategy: preserve theorem/store, theorem/log, and /var/lib/theorem across boot-environment switch
paths.rollback_strategy: activate previous boot environment via beadm/bectl and reboot
paths.operator_guide: Run upgrade.sh from the upgrade-bundle archive on a running 0.6.0 system.

summary: Theorem 0.6.1 ships the full FreeBSD installer alongside the theorem-native BIOS kernel. The FreeBSD stack includes theorem-init, theorem-node, theorem-gateway, theorem-supervisor, and theorem-govagent with rc.d service integration, veriexec integrity manifests, and ZFS boot environments. The bare-metal BIOS kernel provides virtio-net networking, TCP/IP via smoltcp, DHCP with /32 cloud VPS workarounds, and a token-authenticated management server — all in 126 KiB compressed.

artifacts.name: theoremos-v0.6.1-x86_64-unknown-freebsd-upgrade-bundle.tar.gz
artifacts.state_preservation: preserve theorem/store, theorem/log, and /var/lib/theorem across boot-environment switch
